implications: Applicant CV: CV details extensive experience with big data, electronic health records (OpenSAFELY), and implementation science, directly supporting relevant research experience.
Applicant Research Background: Details extensive experience with big data (OpenSAFELY), implementation science, and RCTs, directly aligning with skills required for the proposed study.

Training & Development and Research Support: Confirms prior training in epidemiology and implementation science, and outlines specific future courses (MSc Health Data Science) aligned with project methods.
Applicant CV: CV lists numerous high-impact publications (e.g., The Lancet Rheumatology) and multiple awards (e.g., Eric Bywaters Prize), strongly supporting impactful research outputs.
Applicant Research Background: Lists 28 peer-reviewed publications including 4 first-author in The Lancet, multiple awards, and a guideline cited as the most-cited manuscript in Rheumatology 2023.

Training & Development and Research Support: Host statement cites 28 high-impact publications (Lancet, Annals of Rheumatic Diseases) and awards (BSR Young Investigator), confirming impactful research outputs.
Applicant CV: Progression from trainee to Principal Investigator on NIHR grants and lead analyst roles demonstrates increasing responsibility and trajectory towards leadership.

Applicant Research Background: Demonstrates increasing responsibility through leading national guidelines, managing teams of reviewers, supervising fellows, and developing service pathways implemented nationally.

Training & Development and Research Support: Applicant plans to join NIHR Future-Focused Leadership programme and has taken on teaching/examination roles, demonstrating trajectory towards research leadership.
Applicant CV: CV shows concurrent clinical roles (Rheumatology Specialist Trainee, NIHR Clinical Lecturer) and research positions, confirming a practitioner-academic career path.

Training & Development and Research Support: Applicant requests 20% time for clinical sessions as a consultant rheumatologist, explicitly balancing practice responsibilities with research activities.
